items.title: The Toronto Tempo's inaugural game was a kaleidoscope of emotions
items.summary_auto: The Toronto Tempo's first WNBA regular season game was tight, a kaleidoscope of emotions - everything you could ask for in an inaugural game. The final score may have been a 68-65 loss for the Tempo but it really won’t matter in the long term. Everything about the night was a celebration.

items.title: Has Toronto's condo market hit its bottom?
items.summary_auto: Condo sales picked up in April, as lower prices and borrowing costs helped buyers jump into the market. After years of sky-high prices and sluggish sales, one of the country's most expensive housing markets is seeing something of a condo rebound.

items.title: Celebrities are popping up all over Broadway. Is it making theatre worse?
items.summary_auto: As it becomes more common to see celebrities involved in Broadway, there are both pros and cons about this phenomenon, ultimately shedding light into the financial barriers within the industry along with the diminishing of original new productions.

items.title: A cyberattack hit universities worldwide, including top Canadian schools. Here's what we know
items.summary_auto: Thousands of schools around the globe, including in Canada, have been hit by a massive cybersecurity incident involving Canvas, an online learning-management system that connects millions of students with their instructors.

items.title: CSIS director says Alberta referendum vulnerable to foreign interference
items.summary_auto: The head of Canada's intelligence agency says Alberta's potential secession vote is susceptible to disinformation and foreign interference from players like Russia.

items.title: Britain's Starmer weakened and Labour exposed in local elections
items.summary_auto: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer moved quickly to acknowledge a bruising local election result for his governing Labour Party, appearing before cameras before the final votes were counted in a contest widely seen as a referendum on his leadership.

items.title: A Toronto man could become the 1st Canadian cured of HIV thanks to rare bone marrow treatment
items.summary_auto: A man known as the "Toronto patient" could become the first Canadian cured of HIV. Medical experts say it's a miraculous achievement even if only a small group of people are eligible for the treatment.

items.title: Sidney Morin scores twice to help Frost beat Victoire, force Game 5
items.summary_auto: Defender Sidney Morin scored two goals in the third period, Kelly Pannek added an empty-netter with 1:07 left, and the Minnesota Frost beat the Montreal Victoire 3-1 on Friday night to force a winner-take-all Game 5 in the PWHL playoffs.

items.title: Toronto Tempo fall short of fairy-tale ending in first-ever WNBA regular-season game
items.summary_auto: Shakira Austin sank a pair of free throws with 17 seconds left to play as the Washinton Mystics spoiled the Toronto Tempo's WNBA debut with a 68-65 victory on Friday.

items.title: Toddler rescued from Ottawa River, taken to hospital in critical condition: firefighters
items.summary_auto: A two-year-old child has been taken to hospital in critical condition following a water rescue operation along the Ottawa River, according to a news release from Gatineau Fire Services.

items.title: Ottawa Charge take 2-1 series lead thanks to Savolainen's Game 3 heroics
items.summary_auto: Ronja Savolainen scored the game-winning goal with 29 seconds remaining in regulation to lift the Ottawa Charge to a 2-1 win over the Boston Fleet in Game 3 of their semifinal series on Friday in Ottawa

items.title: Vancouver police jail guard sentenced to house arrest after assaulting detainees
items.summary_auto: A Vancouver Police Department special constable, serving as a jail guard, has been sentenced to six months of house arrest after pleading guilty to assaulting two detainees in January 2023. Omar Ahmed Flores was charged a year after the assaults, one of which involved a 17-year-old Indigenous girl.

items.title: Buble, Morissette among starry lineup at Canadian World Cup opening ceremony
items.summary_auto:
Canadian artists Michael Buble and Alanis
Morissette will be among a star-studded lineup of performers at the
FIFA World Cup opening ceremony in Toronto in June.

items.title: Union wants federal scrutiny of $55B US Electronic Arts sale over national security risks
items.summary_auto: The American company has had a footprint in Canada since at least 1991, when it swallowed Distinctive Software, a Burnaby, B.C.-based video game developer. EA now boasts five offices in the country that have contributed significantly to the company's hit soccer and hockey games and Canada's broader $5.1 billion video game industry.

items.title: Trump releases the UFO files: 5 key questions
items.summary_auto: The U.S. government has made public dozens of previously classified files about unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAPs) — formerly known as UFOs — in what the Trump administration is calling a historic effort at transparency.

items.title: Siblings of child killed by foster parents in 2021 sue B.C. gov't for exposing them to abuse
items.summary_auto: Five years after an 11-year-old Indigenous boy was beaten to death by his foster parents, his five biological and foster siblings are suing the B.C. Ministry of Children and Family Development for exposing them to abuse.

items.title: Ottawa to begin $8.5B rollout for Ontario First Nations to reclaim child welfare
items.summary_auto: Indigenous Services Minister Mandy Gull-Masty says $8.5 billion will begin flowing to Ontario First Nations on May 29 to reclaim their child welfare systems.

items.title: Teen charged with murder of woman, 2 daughters in Brockville, Ont.
Think solving a Rubik's Cube is hard? Try doing it while freefalling from a plane
items.summary_auto: The hardest part about solving a Rubik's Cube while plummeting from an airplane at 200 kilometres per hour is trying not to think about your potential demise, says Tom Kopke. The German YouTuber has beat the Guinness World Record for "fastest time to solve a rotating puzzle cube whilst in freefall."

items.title: Sir David Attenborough turns 100. Here are 10 facts about his life on Earth
items.summary_auto: You know his voice and you definitely know his nature documentaries. But did you know it’s his birthday? Here are 10 facts you might not know about the famed champion for nature — one per decade.

items.title: Optimism competes with anxiety as northern Sask. continues to deal with floods
items.summary_auto: English River First Nation residents are returning after a week of displacement, but over 600 Red Earth Cree Nation members must remain away from home.

items.title: Putin oversees scaled-back WW II Victory Day parade amid temporary Moscow-Kyiv ceasefire
3 men found guilty of 1st-degree murder in 2022 killing of B.C. couple
items.summary_auto: Three men have been found guilty of first-degree murder in the 2022 killing of an Abbotsford couple.

items.title: For Putin, Victory Day has been a time for Russia to project strength. This year, it  will look weaker
items.summary_auto: Russia's Victory Day celebrations have been a chance for President Vladimir Putin to project pride and power, but this year's events have been stripped down amid an onslaught of Ukrainian drone attacks across the country.

items.title: Danielle Smith says working out differences with feds key to quelling Alberta separatism
Russia, Ukraine agree to 3-day ceasefire and prisoner swap announced by Trump
items.summary_auto: Russia and Ukraine confirmed on Friday that they had agreed to a U.S.-brokered three-day ceasefire that will run from May 9 to May 11, and U.S. President Donald Trump said he hoped it ‌would be extended.

items.title: For 2nd time, N.L. judge slams police handling of intimate partner violence cases
items.summary_auto: A Newfoundland and Labrador provincial court judge is once again taking police to task over failing to address intimate partner violence, and says the training the RNC receives when it comes to IPV cases "needs to be reviewed."

items.title: Israeli strikes kill 5 in southern Lebanon as Hezbollah rockets hit open areas in Israel
items.summary_auto: Israeli airstrikes on southern Lebanon killed at least five people Friday, while the Iran-backed Hezbollah militant group fired rockets on northern Israel without inflicting any casualties.
